topic: A close-up of a 'Vinyl Record Player' being started.

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1 [00:00-00:02]: Hand presses the start button on the front panel; front view, close-up.
Shot 2 [00:02-00:05]: Record begins to spin on the platter; top-down.
Shot 3 [00:05-00:08]: Tonearm lowers onto the record; side-angle.
parsed_generation.narration: The sequence tightens on the vinyl record player as it is started. It moves from the start button press to the spinning platter and finally the tonearm lowering to begin playback.

feedback:
The sequence centers on starting a vinyl setup, but the gold standard emphasizes a two-shot macro sequence: a macro shot of the platter as it begins to spin and the tonearm lowering, followed by the needle contact and crackle. To better align, restructure into [00:00-00:04] macro shot of the record on the platter with a hand lowering the tonearm, then [00:04-00:08] the needle contacts groove with a subtle crackle. Include explicit SFX for the crackle to mirror the gold standard and adjust shot timing to match the two-shot cadence.

strengths: Clear shot-by-shot structure with distinct angles (front close-up, top-down, side-angle).
Concise, action-first phrasing that communicates start-up sequence.
fixes: Adopt the gold-standard two-shot structure: [00:00-00:04] macro shot of platter with hand lowering tonearm.
[00:04-00:08] needle contact with groove and a faint crackle SFX to signal playback onset.
